programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The threatened closure of Goddard's following the outbreak of a virus among the children of Skelthwaite prompts Vic to speculate on the real source of the contamination. Meanwhile, Simon and Anna have relationship problems. Rural drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Intrigue and frustration are the order of the day as Anna and Karen come to terms with the possibility that a patient may be suffering domestic abuse. Jess finally gets what's coming to her, while Dick, Cheryl and Oscar struggle to settle into family life. Drama, starring Lesley Dunlop, Leslie Ash and Phil Middlemiss

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe helps the owners of a local Chinese takeaway whose pride and takings are steadily being eroded by racist attacks, while the Great British Grub competition looks set to be a roaring success. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe agrees to cook for a reluctant witness in a police safe house, and comes under threat from both a ruthless villain and an unscrupulous copper. Meanwhile, Henderson is alarmed by an unwelcome reminder of a past indiscretion. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Humiliated that Sir Henry Simmerson has allowed the French to make off with an English standard, Sharpe vows to make amends by capturing the most jealously guarded battlefield prize of them all - a French eagle emblem touched by the hand of Napoleon himself. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley and Brian Cox star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: An alleged sexual assault requires delicate handling. The boat where the attack took place belongs to a friend of the Commissioner, forcing the detectives to go undercover at a fancy dress party as they attempt to arrest their suspect. With only circumstantial evidence and a victim of unreliable character, Ray surprises his colleagues by proving he has a gentler side, gaining the confidence of a young witness

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene comes up against a socialist feminist group when a man's death he initially treated as suicide becomes the subject of a murder inquiry. Chris uses the arrest of every member as an opportunity to impress Shaz with his knowledge of women's issues, while Ray dates a communist. An intriguing code found by Alex and deciphered by the desk sergeant leads the team closer to the truth

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently investigate a consignment of stolen passports, but the case takes an unexpected turn when a woman linked to the crime is found dead on the seashore with her mixed-race baby lying next to her. As the duo follow a lead on the murder victim's ex-boyfriend, they encounter racial prejudice and are drawn into an underground world of gang wars within the Arab community. Primeval's Andrew-Lee Potts guest stars

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike and the team are reacquainted with some familiar faces when investigating a suspicious suicide at the Brokenwood Women's Prison. For Mike this one is personal - he knew the victim Corina Doyle from his days prior to Brokenwood, and with her passing, new information surfaces about an old crime

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The driver of the ladies' motorcar rally team is found dead in her roadster, leaving Phryne struggling to convince Jack that her friend's death was not an accident. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: During a heatwave in Brighton, the Lyell team is summoned to a waste dump where a body part has been found. The search is on to recover the other dismembered remains and piece together the victim's body, which is covered in intricate Japanese tattoos. Away from the lab, Nikki has some news that she desperately wants to share with Matt, but must wait until he visits from America

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jim and the team investigate when a dismembered body is found under the floorboards of a Glasgow house. Things take a turn for the worse when the victim's nine-month-old baby is kidnapped from a multi-storey car park. Drama, starring Mark McManus, Neil Duncan, Harriet Buchan and Robert Robertson

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Australian drama about a hedonistic heart surgeon, Dr Hugh Knight, who is forced to work as a rural GP in his hometown of Whyhope. Starring Rodger Corser

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor unwittingly saves the life of a brutal East End villain, who begins terrorising patients and staff alike. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William decides to complain to the mayor, after he and Hester are kept awake by the nocturnal activities of a local farmer. Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan takes a shine to his flirtatious 'girlfriend' when he goes undercover in a sting operation to entrap a fraudulent currency dealer - and turns up late for his first serious outing in the boxing ring. Stuart and Sam investigate an arson attack on the home of a foster carer which seems to be the work of a disgruntled former charge. June finally hears the news she's been waiting for from Rod

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gillian Taylforth joins the cast as Nikki Wright, a Sergeant from the Barton Street station who finds herself caught up in an armed stand-off along with her Sun Hill colleagues. Her quick thinking defuses a tense situation and earns the praise of visiting DAC Georgia Hobbs, who recommends her for a transfer. James Tennant's life continues to fall apart as he is arrested for drink driving and assault

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gillian Taylforth joins the cast as Nikki Wright, a Sergeant from the Barton Street station who finds herself caught up in an armed stand-off along with her Sun Hill colleagues. Her quick thinking defuses a tense situation and earns the praise of visiting DAC Georgia Hobbs, who recommends her for a transfer. James Tennant's life continues to fall apart as he is arrested for drink driving and assault

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates an assault on a prostitute and ends up offering her a room for the night, ignoring Jack's advice to avoid emotional involvement. Later in the day, he bumps into ex-girlfriend Mia and finally manages to talk to her about his rape, which has left him unable to form close bonds with people. Yvonne makes progress in the case against GP Andrew Wilder and Dan's girlfriend dumps him in humiliating circumstances

programme.title: The Wind in the Willows
programme.desc: Lavish live-action adaptation of Kenneth Grahame's much-loved children's novel. Matt Lucas stars as Toad, the aristocratic amphibian with a love of fast cars, with Bob Hoskins, Mark Gatiss and Lee Ingleby as his best friends Badger, Rat and Mole, who 'poop-poop' his new hobby when they realise he is becoming a menace to other road-users. However, Mr Toad ignores them, runs away and steals a motor car - for which he gets thrown into prison for 20 years. With no one to look after his stately manor house, the place is overrun with stoats and weasels

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part special. The women of Cranford are excited about the imminent visit of Lady Glenmire, until the Hon Mrs Jamieson announces that none of them is of high enough social standing to meet her - so Miss Matty leads the Amazons in snubbing both the newcomer and her hostess. But sociable Lady Glenmire is quick to offer an olive branch by hosting a memorable Halloween party, which results in the forging of new friendships. Mr Buxton is impressed by Edward Bell's ambitions, so takes him on as land agent, only for the new employee to make a disastrous decision that has dire consequences for both himself and his sister Peggy. Meanwhile, Matty is distressed when a surprising discovery about one of her close circle results in a serious falling out among the friends, and when tragedy strikes the town, she fears Cranford may never recover. Judi Dench, Celia Imrie and Jonathan Pryce star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Dwelling Place
programme.desc: Feature-length adaptation of the best-selling novel, telling the tale of a 16-year-old struggling to keep her five brothers and sisters out of the workhouse with the help of a kindly carpenter. Starring James Fox, Tracy Whitwell and Edward Rawle-Hicks

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives of Nonnatus House return, with new nurse Barbara, who is anxious to prove herself - only to get off on the wrong foot after a late-night drinks party. A family of severely neglected children is discovered, so Trixie strives to restore their health and bring happiness into their lives, leaving Tom filled with admiration for the woman he loves, while Sister Evangelina finally undergoes tests for her abdominal pains. Shelagh finds she is at the surgery almost as much as she is at home, so the Turners come up with a solution which, for 1960, proves somewhat ground-breaking

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Adaptation of Victor Hugo's novel, which follows an epic journey through 19th-century France as the nation stands on the brink of revolution. Dominic West plays the lead role of Jean Valjean, a former convict attempting to begin his life anew while all around him a desperate underclass struggles to survive. Valjean's search for a fresh start is thwarted by prejudice against ex-convicts and the suspicion of an obsessed former guard who is convinced he will reoffend. Elsewhere, an army officer returns home from the battle of Waterloo desperate to reconnect with his son, while a seamstress has an encounter that could lead to her downfall. With David Oyelowo, Lily Collins and David Bradley

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The maverick detective finds his career on the line once more when an apparently watertight murder prosecution collapses because of his unconventional interrogation methods. Facing the wrath of the victim's family - not to mention his own colleagues - Rebus digs deeper, but finds himself caught up in a spate of gangland revenge killings. Nicholas Farrell guest stars, with Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Pat is forced to deal with a persistent complainer who has been hounding the station, and becomes concerned when he starts stalking her. Eventually Pat is abducted by Warrington and forced to discover Cassie North's corpse. Drama, starring Kate Buffery and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh receives a shocking introduction to Outback medicine when he attends a farm accident. Drama, starring Rodger Corser

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant makes a confession to Nigel, but leaves Phil to speculate about his plans. Sharon takes a last look at the world she grew up in

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina has her hands full when a high-powered visitor decides to take a tour of the station - where Terry is nursing his wounds from love rival David Coles and the custody office has become a crime scene after a rowdy domestic turns into a stabbing incident. The beleaguered Inspector comes up smiling when the eligible bigwig asks her out on a date. Linda Robson and Nicola Stapleton guest star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chrissie decides she no longer wants to be a nurse and feels able to offer only moral rather than medical assistance to a boy injured in a car accident. Connie realises she can never break up John's family and ends her relationship with him, all the while fighting to save his son's life. Joseph hopes that bringing the wedding forward will end Faye's insecurity over his desire to be a father

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A new recruit arrives, but someone else has already turned up claiming to be the same person, leaving Tess with a mystery to solve. Comfort struggles to cope with memories of the train crash, prompting her to question her religious beliefs. Keith refuses to accept Claire's answer to his marriage proposal. Josh finds himself in hot water after making bad decisions at the scene of an accident

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will gets involved in an affray outside a nightclub and finds his career is on the line - even though the incident gives the team enough evidence to foil a raid on a jeweller's shop. Zain's father is arrested on suspicion of burglary, but it turns out he is on a more high-minded mission on behalf of a friend. After a night of passion, Gina discovers her new conquest is a married man

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant gets everyone to go to Quiz Night. Liam tries to get Clare away from Nigel, Sharon tells Pauline about Grant, David cannot cope with his feelings for Bianca

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack heads out around town with Sarah on her first day at work, but the horrors of the war cast a shadow over Inverness. Meanwhile, Olivia is unable to stop herself from wondering about the woman James admitted to loving in Europe, and makes a shocking discovery. Drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp, Craig Hall, Arianwen Parkes-Lockwood and David Berry

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew and Kate offer moral support to an alcoholic woman fighting to keep custody of her children, and find themselves growing closer together in the process. Meanwhile, over at the Black Swan, Mike and Liz try to digest unexpected news. Gary Mavers, Melanie Kilburn, John Bowler and Lynsey Baxter star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Flamboyant vet Granville Bennett makes an impression on James with his generosity. Rural drama, guest starring James Grout (Inspector Morse), with Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William decides to complain to the mayor, after he and Hester are kept awake by the nocturnal activities of a local farmer. Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tests confirm Tom has prostate trouble and Harvey is excited when a representative from Bayview has been chosen to attend the Council of European Old People. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: As Michelle makes arrangements to send a microfilm to England by homing pigeon, Herr Flick captures Yvette and Mimi in order to gain access to the cafe to search for the painting of the Fallen Madonna. However, the town's annual Fishmongers' Parade proves a hindrance for all concerned. Gordon Kaye and Kirsten Cooke star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry buys Glenda a chaise longue from Auntie Wainwright's shop as an anniversary present, but it soon proves to be more than just something to sit on. Meanwhile, Howard finds himself locked in Pearl's bedroom with no means of escape, so Truly, Clegg and Billy set out on a rescue mission. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Mike Grady

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal O'Hanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JP's past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back home in Bermondsey for his youngest daughter's wedding, Gerry is visited by Ralph Paxton, an old friend recently released from prison who wants him investigate the murder of his grandson. Meanwhile, a newly divorced Sasha is forced to work with ex-husband Ned Hancock, Danny puts the body language skills he has acquired on an FBI course to the test, and Gerry's daughter Caitlin gets upset when her father interrogates her fiance about his credentials. Cold case drama, starring Dennis Waterman, Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst, with a guest appearance by David Hayman (Trial & Retribution)

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A former spy visits Thames House - but it soon becomes clear she has more on her mind than catching up with old friends. Armed with a bomb, she holds the team hostage - demanding its members produce evidence that the security services assassinated Diana, Princess of Wales. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan, with Peter Firth, Rupert Penry-Jones and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The NFL Show

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Buckfast Abbey, where items brought in to be valued include a diamond tiara, a collection of First World War medals, and some miniature furniture

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Peter is shocked when Duncan calls with news that scandalous photos of his youngest daughter Lily are about to hit the newspapers, while suspicions arise within his family that he has not been entirely faithful. In Washington DC, Charmian Pepper resumes her investigation into Peter's dealings and makes a breakthrough - but with terrible consequences

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium, as the Foxes looked to avenge a defeat at the hands of the Gunners at King Power Stadium in the Carabao Cup. Plus, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of novelist Andrea Levy, charting her journey to become one of the country's best loved contemporary novelists. He looks back at her best-selling novel, Small Island, which captured the imagination of readers around the world, and her most recent novel, The Long Song, has recently been adapted by the BBC, featuring an all-star cast

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three entrepreneurs to watch customers test and score a dry shampoo, a measuring tool and a clamp for wheelie-bin lids

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack seeks parenting advice from a grief-stricken Mitch, who tells him to make sure he puts the kids first. Max offers Linda work at the restaurant, but when she asks Mick for his thoughts, he seems indifferent - angering Tina with his irritable behaviour. Sonia is ecstatic to receive a car as a gift from one of her recent patients, while Bobby chooses his trustees for the Lucy Beale Foundation

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine footage of an octopus that thinks it is Houdini, a stoat that hypnotises its prey, and a massive lake in America that disappeared

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Featuring a Korean rocket festival and solar-powered supertrees in Singapore

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Adam attempts some dog training techniques with Henry, while vet Rory is in studio to show how to make your own doggy treats at home. And we hear how BP fans have been celebrating Black History Month.

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and Wallace book a holiday to the Costa Del Generica

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia must act fast as Wallace becomes possessed by an evil toaster

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's on a quest to find the deadliest prehistoric aquatic monster

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the second of two programmes in which pooches and pussies are put through a number of tests to see which family pet comes out on top. There are five more rounds, concentrating on which animal is easiest to train, communication, which is best at helping reduce people's stress, independence and the big question - do cats and dogs really love their owners? On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris meets a Border Collie which can recognise more than 150 words and Liz meets a cat capable of choosing its own TV channels

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon host the revival of the programme in which radio-controlled robots created by teams of amateur engineers battle it out in an arena. The machines have to make it through an initial group stage, then head-to-head rounds before the heat final. The first show features students from Reading and a family team from Caithness, as well as a memorable robot from the original series

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry

programme.title: A Star Is Born (1954)
programme.desc: A Hollywood movie star with a drinking problem is captivated by an aspiring singer's performance. He falls in love with her and helps her become a star, but as her career blossoms, his falls into ruin. Musical drama, starring Judy Garland, James Mason, Jack Carson and Charles Bickford. Directed by George Cukor, with songs by Harold Arlen and Ira Gershwin

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by Claire Rawle and James Lewis at St Albans Cathedral, where items include some historic coins and a 1970s robot

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: Jamie Crawford takes to the skies to explore Scotland's cities, coasts and countryside, using aerial images to uncover fascinating stories from Scotland's past. On the island of Canna, Jamie scrambles down a cliff-face to uncover an ancient Christian building, and in the north-east he takes a plunge in a fabulous but freezing outdoor swimming pool

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon plan to stage a re-enactment of the St George and the dragon story as part of the Wings for Victory fundraising campaign, but discover Hodges and his fellow ARP wardens have had the same idea. Vintage comedy first shown in 1977, starring Arthur Lowe

programme.title: Maggi Hambling: Making Love with the Paint
programme.desc: Painter and sculptor Maggi Hambling allows cameras into her rural Suffolk studio for the first time, talking to film-maker Randall Wright about her life and career in a film to mark her 75th birthday. Formidable and funny, Maggi recalls her artistic liberation during her student days, a memorable meeting with Francis Bacon in Soho, and falling in love with his muse Henrietta Moraes, a woman Maggi continues to draw from memory following her death. With insights from Maggi's partner and fellow artist Tory Lawrence, colleague Sarah Lucas and art writer James Cahill

programme.title: Apostasy (2017)
programme.desc: A Jehovah's Witness is forced to shun her own sister because of a religious contravention. As the separation drags on, she starts to question the meaning of God's love. Drama, starring Siobhan Finneran, Robert Emms, Bronwyn James

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. Barry White's deep baritone also put both black and white audiences 'in the mood'. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary crew follows the daily lives of four vampires sharing a house in New Zealand as they prepare for an annual gathering of the undead. A new member of the household introduces the bloodsuckers to modern life, but his failure to follow the laws of their kind brings his flatmates nothing but trouble. Horror comedy, directed by and starring Jemaine Clement and Taika Waititi

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter L as she continues her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don gives tips on how to look after wildlife in the months ahead, shows how to look after and protect tender garden plants and plans ahead for next year's harvest of fruit. Joe Swift finds out what it takes to look after a historic garden with a collection of heritage fruit trees in Hampshire, and Arit Anderson meets Fergus Garrett - head gardener at Great Dixter - to discover how the planting and garden management have increased biodiversity. Plus, the Staffordshire couple with a particular fondness for Japanese maples

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith investigates an uncertain future for upland sheep producers, while Anne Lundon discovers Scotland's Tree of the Year. Arlene Stuart heads to Rannoch in Perthshire to meet an inspiring man who is determined to access the countryside despite his disability

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Visiting gardens and people who see the garden as an artist's palette, from a topiary zoo to a woman who makes her art out of other people's rubbish. Plus, a visit to EA Hornell's garden in Kirkcudbright

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the final episode, Nadiya wraps up the series with a selection of recipes for special occasions, including an eight-layered birthday cake, a sparkly Mardi Gras king cake, hot cross buns, and a spectacularly festive biscuit tower

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the potato, preparing a Lancashire potato and butter pie and a chicken casserole and cobbler to show it at its best. They see how British vodka made out of the vegetable is winning global awards and reflect on a dark time in Welsh history when the chip shops almost ran out of chips

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver meets a photographer who covered John F Kennedy's visit to Galway in June 1963 as the team explores Ireland's Atlantic coast, and learns about pirate queen Grace O'Malley. Dick Strawbridge tries to re-create Marconi's radio technology, Miranda Krestovnikoff examines why mountain hares are eating seaweed, Alice Roberts discovers the oldest farm in the British Isles, dating back to the Stone Age, and Nick Crane investigates Clew Bay's landscape

programme.title: The Long Ships (1963)
programme.desc: A Viking warrior steals a ship and leads his crew in search of a legendary bell, rumoured to be three times the size of a man and made of solid gold. But they have reckoned without the ambition of the king of the Moors, who captures the Vikings and intends to use them to find the prize for himself. Adventure, starring Richard Widmark, Sidney Poitier, Rosanna Schiaffino, Russ Tamblyn and Lionel Jeffries

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at popular black stars of the cinema and Hollywood's role in the civil rights movement, including interviews with Sidney Poitier, Denzel Washington and Morgan Freeman

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and author David Walliams delves into his family history, learning of his paternal great-grandfather's traumatic experiences during the First World War and unearthing the story of an ancestor who became blind as the result of pioneering eye surgery in the 1880s

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman investigate the origins and production of more household staples, beginning with tea. Gregg looks at the factory that produces one quarter of all the tea drunk in Britain. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey examines an African tea processing plant, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the profit-making potential of a father and son team's colourful wetsuits, two former politicians with ideas about alternative energy, a couple's African coffee ceremony business, and a fake tan product with a creator who has already braved the Irish version of the show

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael reflects on his fourth travel series, exploring the Sahara. This journey brought him into contact with nomads, refugees and artists who had sought out this vast desert, but also those displaced by war, the migrant crisis and the rise of militant Islam. The journey took Michael over the Atlas Mountains, to a Smara refugee camp, to the famed city of Timbuktu, and into the deep desert as part of a camel train

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at the campaign to bring the slave trade to an end after 400 years. Divers explore the sunken remains of a ship that had been carrying freed slaves who had been recaptured. Having traced his own ancestry back to the Benga people of Gabon, Samuel visits the country to learn more of their traditions and stories. Plus, a trip to Costa Rica in search of evidence of escapees from two Danish slave ships

programme.title: Loving (2016)
programme.desc: In late-1950s Virginia, interracial couple Richard and Mildred Loving are targeted by the authorities and arrested, leading to a momentous legal battle that would end with the Supreme Court's historic 1967 decision. Director Jeff Nichols' critically acclaimed fact-based drama, starring an Oscar-nominated Ruth Negga, with Joel Edgerton, Will Dalton and Michael Shannon

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Kensei's power is revealed, while Suresh discovers the location of the Haitian and manages to cure him. Hiro tries to minimise the damage caused by his journey into the past, and Peter joins a group of Irish gangsters who are familiar with his history. Meanwhile, Matt searches for clues to the identity of Mr Nakamura's killer, and West learns of Claire's extraordinary abilities

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Suresh finds an unknown Isaac Mendez painting portraying someone's death. Maya breaks Alejandro out of a Mexican jail, and Hiro discovers something about Kensei. Meanwhile, Niki and Micah flee Las Vegas in an attempt to outrun their past, while Claire joins West on a trip. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary analyses the worlds of architecture and fashion during the pandemic, noting that while the clothes people wear have already changed, cities and homes need to change to accommodate new ways of living. She also compares the new normal with life in ancient Rome, and fulfils a 40-year ambition by reuniting three cast members of 1970s BBC drama I, Claudius - Derek Jacobi, Sian Phillips and Brian Blessed

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Lolly Adefope and Ellie Taylor, musician Ricky Wilson and presenter and author Richard Osman, who reveal the books that make them cry and the fictional characters they most relate to. They discuss this week's book group picks - Love After Love by Ingrid Persaud and Richard's debut novel, The Thursday Murder Club

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete as veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a drop-leaf table, an old wingback chair and a collection of metal baskets from the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Westminster, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights of the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Leeds Rhinos, Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils and Catalans Dragons v Hull FC

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, Melissa Downhill and Micaela Sharp head to Hay-on-Wye in Powys, where they have a day to find bargains they can turn into a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Now they are officially an item, Fiona and George decide to host a dinner party for the whole of Weld, but George doesn't have a big enough table. In fact, they soon realise they may have bitten off a little more than they can chew and this might have come a bit early for both of them

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants are asked to insert an invisible zip and pockets on a pair of palazzo pants, while the judges keep a keen eye on how well they pattern-match the bold prints. For the transformation challenge, the sewers must turn towels into stylish beach cover-ups in just 90 minutes. Male models arrive in the Sewing Room for the final challenge, ready to be fitted with a made-to-measure holiday shirt using bright and colourful fabrics. Whose shirt will bring on the sunshine, and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presenter Paul Martin and experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and James Lewis visit Bristol Cathedral, where Natasha is intrigued by an unusual walking stick with a top made from a German Zeppelin, and James comes across a collection of lead soldiers. Paul learns about the origins of Bristol's vibrant street art scene and has a go at making a graffiti stencil.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal return with the fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, looking at all the news and gossip from the first weekend of the competition

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark chapman presents coverage of the draw, as the non-league clubs to have progressed to the first-round and the teams from League One and Two await their fate

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Montgomery bus boycott, The Crown, the history of anaesthesia, and Stormzy, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Dungeons and Dragons players the Dungeon Masters take on Welsh border team the Severns. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Bottersnikes, Wayne Szalinski's kids, Mrs Pepperpot, and Ant-Man

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the second and final of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two sets of students get a last opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show from his garage. A diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined remotely by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A call comes in from London Fire Brigade requesting assistance at a fire in a block of flats which immediately puts the team under pressure. Meanwhile, mother and daughter team Nicola and Maisie have just finished with their first patient of the night but are immediately dispatched to a 12-year-old boy who has had an unfortunate accident

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Chris Beardshaw and wife Frances take on a new allotment and are starting from scratch to create an easy, low-maintenance fruit and vegetable patch. Kirsty Wilson is looking to extend the season by using late-flowering perennials that complement autumn colours and Brian Cunningham takes advantage of the season to plant cost-effective bare root trees. To conclude the season, the team enjoys a video call - including a surprise visitor to the party

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto investigates the truth behind the sinking of Venice and discusses the likely long-term effect of a current exodus of inhabitants leaving to find jobs elsewhere. He explains how 19th-century British artists and writers, including Turner and Dickens, wove a romantic myth around the city, and describes his family's connection to one sexually promiscuous figure

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Travelling through Vietnam, Michael stops off along the way to witness the changing face of Hanoi before boarding the Reunification Express for Saigon - an emotionally charged journey which throws up many memories of the Vietnam War. In the Philippines, he watches a psychic surgeon at work, but ends the programme on a lighter note by helping judge the Miss Bella Pacifica contest

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf Larsen, a respected detective on the Copenhagen police force, investigates the case of a missing child, but his life is brutally upended when his own baby daughter goes missing. Five years after the event, a new lead emerges when a serious flaw is uncovered in the police's DNA database. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: A stolen car in Thornby results in Rolf meeting young police assistant Neel and the detective is reluctantly ordered by their joint boss to test the car thief's DNA with his old colleagues in Copenhagen. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on February 2, with performances by artists including Sybil, Eurythmics, Skid Row, Cher, Sinead O'Connor and the House of Love

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on February 8, with performances by artists including Phil Collins, the Beloved, Janet Jackson, Del Amitri and Beats International featuring Lindy Layton

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga and Henrik interview the estranged sister of the latest victim, a teacher who had been on the receiving end of complaints from his pupils. Lillian discovers an unusual injury on the inside of a hospitalised Hans' cheek, and Claus encounters Anneka while attending the Freddie Holst's gallery opening. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: A young art curator approaches Henrik and Saga, claiming to have spotted a connection between the murders of Helle Anker and Lars-Ove Abrahamsson, and the assault on Hans. While the detectives remain sceptical about his suggestions, they interview Freddie Holst with this new theory in mind. However, the case takes another gruesome turn when another victim is found with similar mutilations. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto investigates the truth behind the sinking of Venice and discusses the likely long-term effect of a current exodus of inhabitants leaving to find jobs elsewhere. He explains how 19th-century British artists and writers, including Turner and Dickens, wove a romantic myth around the city, and describes his family's connection to one sexually promiscuous figure

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Travelling through Vietnam, Michael stops off along the way to witness the changing face of Hanoi before boarding the Reunification Express for Saigon - an emotionally charged journey which throws up many memories of the Vietnam War. In the Philippines, he watches a psychic surgeon at work, but ends the programme on a lighter note by helping judge the Miss Bella Pacifica contest

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: Using the movie archives of three farming families from the east of England, the documentary focuses on wheat growing and explains how Britain became self-sufficient in bread-making after the Second World War. Their stories reveal how land workers progressed from horse to machine power, and employed science and genetics to improve yields

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: An early dusting of snow signals the end of autumn, a time when the forests are a blaze of red and gold and the animals prepare for the coming cold. The elk face hungry wolves as they move down from the mountains to feed in the valleys, while beavers rush to defend their habitat against the destructive ice, repairing dams and stocking underwater larders before the waters freeze over

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor begins his trek across the Sahara Desert. Landing in Morocco after crossing the Straits of Gibraltar, he proceeds to scale the High Atlas mountains and meets the Sahawari people, who provide welcome assistance in an inhospitable land. He also rides on the world's longest train, and plays a game of desert draughts

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The former Monty Python star's expedition takes him to Senegal, where he witnesses a wrestling competition, visits jazz clubs, and meets soap star Marie-Madeleine. He also visits Bamako and Dogon country, then travels to Djenne, where he celebrates the Muslim Tabaski feast and secures a passage on a cargo boat to take him down the Niger River to Timbuktu

programme.title: Frankenstein: How to Make a Monster
programme.desc: In BAC Beatbox Academy's hit show, six performers interpret Mary Shelley's novel Frankenstein from their own perspective as young people growing up in 21st-century Britain. This musical film is part performance, part documentary, with the cast's voices as the only instruments

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Anatomist Alice Roberts explores Mary Shelley's Frankenstein, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking who could have influenced the author's vision of the story. Plus, the presenter travels to the Villa Diodati in Geneva, where Shelley first conceived the idea of Victor Frankenstein's creature, before examining the original manuscript at the Bodleian Library in Oxford

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy takes drastic action when conflict in the Mitchell household threatens to shake the fragile peace. And Dot might discover the real Clare

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone is shocked when Phil takes his feelings out on Ben, Suzy has something to hide, and Ian makes a discovery about Peter

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Archie becomes the hero of the day, Sean learns about Tracey's curse of the Vic, Ronnie discovers Max's dark side, and Dot receives bad news about Clare

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dot is adamant that she cannot face Jim coming home for his birthday, Bianca and family come back from their holiday, and the Masoods deal with Ian's bribe

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: Using the movie archives of three farming families from the east of England, the documentary focuses on wheat growing and explains how Britain became self-sufficient in bread-making after the Second World War. Their stories reveal how land workers progressed from horse to machine power, and employed science and genetics to improve yields

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred visits the North East, a region rich in transport history, to explore the development of Britain's railways, from the first steam locomotives to the record-breaking Mallard. A visit to York's National Railway Museum offers the chance to ride a replica of Stephenson's Rocket, while at Ffestiniog Railway he learns about the computer-aided design process used for new locomotives

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a cabin in a winter wonderland, complete with soft hills bedecked in purple

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould visit a B&B in Aberdeenshire to pick up where the owner's late husband left off in proving that a painting is the work of 19th-century French artist Paul Delaroche. A visit to the British museum raises evidence that this could be a lost treasure once belonging to France's last king and queen, and they head for Normandy to inspect a stained glass window in the image of a lost Delaroche painting - but they also begin to suspect it is a copy, rather than the original

programme.title: Michael Palin's Quest for Artemisia
programme.desc: The Monty Python star sets off on a quest to investigate the story of little-known 17th-century Italian artist Artemisia Gentileschi after one of her paintings caught his eye. Michael encounters her work in Florence, Rome and Naples, and finds out about her life as a working mother and her ultimate success against all odds as a great painter of the Baroque age who transformed the way women were depicted in art, and who was sought after in many courts across Europe

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how a cute cartoon character became an international hate symbol after being hijacked by the alt-right, following Pepe's creator Matt Furie as he fights to bring back his lovable comic-book character from the dark forces who stole him

programme.title: How to Go Viral: The Art of the Meme with Richard Clay
programme.desc: Art historian Professor Richard Clay explores how internet memes are shaping culture and politics, and experiments by devising and releasing some himself

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mr Malone holds a mock trial to resolve a dispute Martha and Ollie are having over the ownership of Edison, a cuddly lightbulb they won together at a fairground hoopla. Rufus is appointed Ollie’s ineffective lawyer. (His amenability is something his girlfriend Kat finds very frustrating). Lily is Martha’s disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha objects, but Judge Kat insists that it will be amusing to witness Martha throw hoops. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. Later, whilst Rufus admits to Kat that he doesn’t feel he is cut out to be a lawyer, he offers Kat the largest slice of cake even though he wants it. This infuriates Kat. This time the team take part in Ely's eel-throwing competition, journey to Land's End in Cornwall where they learn how to make a true Cornish pasty, explore Luton's top attractions with Game Show Man and mystified locals, visit Northern Ireland to discover the connection between Star Wars porgs and Rathlin Island puffins and pay tribute to Dolly Parton on a taxi tour of London, while Holly H reveals some weird facts about the UK’s capital city. Ed is joined on this amazing and offbeat road trip by Sam and Mark, Lindsey Russell, Naomi Wilkinson, Annabelle Davis, Max and Harvey, Joe Tasker, The Mandeville Sisters and Holly H - bet you didn’t know this stuff was in the UK, but it turns up All Over The Place!

programme.title: Billionaire Boy ()
programme.desc: Family comedy, by David Walliams and Kevin Cecil. Len Spud and his 12-year-old son Joe become billionaires when Len invents a new toilet roll. Len soon starts dating fortysomething hand model Sapphire Diamond, while the butler helps a neglected and unhappy Joe transfer to the local comprehensive where he conceals the facts of his wealth. However, when Len turns up at the school with his son's forgotten homework, the cat is soon out of the bag. John Thomson stars as the inventor, alongside Walliams, Catherine Tate and Rebecca Front, and featuring Warwick Davis and Bryn Terfel as themselves

programme.desc: Formula One superstar Lewis Hamilton joins three black teenagers on a mission to make life better for black kids growing up in the UK. programme.desc: In this episode, Dr Maggie takes us into space to study Earth and see how it has helped us to understand climate change. All your space questions are answered in Ask Maggie, and she counts down the top five surprising space facts.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Determined not to let herself or her country down, will Iona master the surf - and the pressure - to come home a champion?

programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.

But soon she finds out that everything at school is a little bit different and not the same. The school smells funny, the desks are different and worst of all, its not Mrs Hanson its Mrs Shorring! As the weather starts to change Lola discovers all kinds of things about Autumn are different - and she doesn't like it one bit. To cap it all, her big Autumn picture goes all wrong. programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is relaxing at home, playing games and watching television. She shows us how a television screen works using millions of tiny squares called pixels. Using her special microscope camera, Maddie shows us how a pixel can make any colour from just three coloured lights - red, blue and green. Next she visits a factory where carpet is made. Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Maddie uses her special slow-motion camera to show us how the different coloured threads are woven together on a loom to make a carpet.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: A big storm wrecks the rabbits' treehouse, and placing it back together leads to a big adventure

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It’s Make Something Epic Day and Love Monster knows exactly what he is going to make. In a fantasy, he imagines standing at the top of an epic fort in the forest while his friends come to play with him. Out of the fantasy, Love Monster gets to work on a building using rocks, twigs and wood from the forest. But when he has finished building, the fort that he has made isn’t epic at all. It’s just a pile of rocks, twigs and wood. He realises that this might be a bigger job than he thought.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that go off-road

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Searching in North America for a Stegosaurus - a plant eater that was as long as a double decker bus. Presented by Andy Day

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Purl have started the Amazing Owl Club, with its own rulebook, a members-only clubhouse and - best of all - a special badge with flapping owl wings

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The cartoon heroes spring into action when Glitch's mini-master toys invade Hong Kong Harbour

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Waffle, Ruby and Jelly have a playdate, and Mrs Hobbs has a delivery shop disaster

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo discovers that Gran Gran used to be in a band with Grandad and decides that she wants to learn how to play the drums. Gran Gran teaches JoJo how to play to a beat ,and eventually they form a new band with Jared and Cynthia called JoJo and the Jammers!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The pair attempt to take photographs of birds after being inspired by a nature documentary

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Everybody is quietly reading books when the little toy tub appears

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka makes a pile of stones and decides to give one to each of the garden's inhabitants as a present - but finds he still has one left over

programme.title: Hushabye Lullabye
programme.desc: Dillie Dally plays amongst the autumn leaves as they twirl and whirl and fall around him – can Dillie Dally find the hidden Chumbles?

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: JB Gill reads Families, Families, Families! by Suzanne and Max Lang

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple search for a new home in South Wales, big enough to house their huge collection of superhero memorabilia

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Katie Keat attempts to bring a wooden swivel seat back to its best, while Sarah has a collection of cutlery to find a use for

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: In the quiet fishing village of Porthgain, Pembrokeshire, Derek Brockway meets local seaman Captain Brian Thomas, who spent weeks in a coma after collapsing while out walking. The pair explore the area's industrial past and soak in the stunning sea views. Then, at RSPB Conwy, the presenter meets Nicola Cockburn who has been blind since birth, to find out what considerations are involved when she goes out walking and how she manages to indulge her passion of bird-watching

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special current affairs edition of the general knowledge quiz. Featuring John Pienaar, Ayesha Hazarika, Iain Dale, Jacqui Smith, Camilla Tominey, Rachel Johnson, Martin Bell and Michael Fabricant

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the chosen six bands make their debuts, competing to impress and performing head to head for the very first time, with only five bands making it through to the next round

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Nat Coombs is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to review Thursday's game between Philadelphia Eagles and New York Giants, plus a look ahead to the other fixtures in week seven

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.desc: Two teams compete at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthen, where presenter Charlie Ross is joined by experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: To mark Black History Month, guest presenter Mark De-Lisser visits the International Slavery Museum in Liverpool, and YolanDa Brown meets Christian classical pianist Alexis Ffrench
