programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Maxine Peake presents an appeal on behalf of Jessie's Fund, a UK charity helping disabled and seriously ill children through the use of music

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following residents of a pioneering Welsh eco-village Brithdir Mawr, who are attempting to raise £1million to save their homes and their way of life before the lease expires. The documentary follows the community over the course of a year as they try to save their homes and preserve the eco-hamlet for posterity

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Samantha Buca helps a burlesque dancer to overcome her anxiety by designing a showstopper of a dress, and also helps a bride whose mother is struggling to come to terms with her daughter marrying an older man. Finally, the bridesmaids for a woman marrying into a large family try on dresses

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The mothers and daughters spend most of their day washing, baking and cleaning, while the fathers and sons are exhausted by their work at the coal face - but the end of the experience is in sight and the participants will soon get to return to 21st-century life

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Buckfast Abbey, where items brought in to be valued include a diamond tiara, a collection of First World War medals, and some miniature furniture

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Peter is shocked when Duncan calls with news that scandalous photos of his youngest daughter Lily are about to hit the newspapers, while suspicions arise within his family that he has not been entirely faithful. In Washington DC, Charmian Pepper resumes her investigation into Peter's dealings and makes a breakthrough - but with terrible consequences

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium, as the Foxes looked to avenge a defeat at the hands of the Gunners at King Power Stadium in the Carabao Cup. Plus, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of novelist Andrea Levy, charting her journey to become one of the country's best loved contemporary novelists. He looks back at her best-selling novel, Small Island, which captured the imagination of readers around the world, and her most recent novel, The Long Song, has recently been adapted by the BBC, featuring an all-star cast

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host a weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical content and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Bilston, Wolverhampton, Bignall End in Staffordshire and Brimrod in Rochdale

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers raid a Birmingham sweatshop suspected of being a major supplier of counterfeit clothing, and pursue a builder who rendered a family home unfit to be lived in

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Featuring the rescue of a hiker who was searching for his dog when a cliff crumbled beneath his feet, while passers-by save the life of a woman who collapsed in the street

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three entrepreneurs to watch customers test and score a dry shampoo, a measuring tool and a clamp for wheelie-bin lids

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The case of a 19-year-old who was scammed out of his student loan. Plus, a look at how pet scams and thefts have soared during the pandemic, and advice on protecting against fraud

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack seeks parenting advice from a grief-stricken Mitch, who tells him to make sure he puts the kids first. Max offers Linda work at the restaurant, but when she asks Mick for his thoughts, he seems indifferent - angering Tina with his irritable behaviour. Sonia is ecstatic to receive a car as a gift from one of her recent patients, while Bobby chooses his trustees for the Lucy Beale Foundation

programme.title: Black and Welsh
programme.desc: Cardiff-born film-maker Liana Rose Stewart brings together people from across Wales to share their experiences of what it means to be black and Welsh, hearing from actors, comedians, leading business figures, influencers and heroes of their communities

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. Sonia is ecstatic to receive a car as a gift from one of her recent patients, while Bobby chooses his trustees for the Lucy Beale Foundation

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sara Pascoe. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on. She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall recycling centre on a mission to save three unwanted items, rescuing a well-used table, a retro chair and rusty gate rods from the skip

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special current affairs edition of the general knowledge quiz. Featuring John Pienaar, Ayesha Hazarika, Iain Dale, Jacqui Smith, Camilla Tominey, Rachel Johnson, Martin Bell and Michael Fabricant

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the chosen six bands make their debuts, competing to impress and performing head to head for the very first time, with only five bands making it through to the next round

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Nat Coombs is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to review Thursday's game between Philadelphia Eagles and New York Giants, plus a look ahead to the other fixtures in week seven

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A family consider moving to Australia to help their son, who has a rare genetic disorder. Though mum Jayne believes sunshine will help young Albie, dad Eugene needs convincing

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams compete at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthen, where presenter Charlie Ross is joined by experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: To mark Black History Month, guest presenter Mark De-Lisser visits the International Slavery Museum in Liverpool, and YolanDa Brown meets Christian classical pianist Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Maxine Peake presents an appeal on behalf of Jessie's Fund, a UK charity helping disabled and seriously ill children through the use of music

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues three items about to be dumped at an Altrincham tip, with the experts transforming a table, a record player and a farmhouse chair

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Buckfast Abbey, where items brought in to be valued include a diamond tiara, a collection of First World War medals, and some miniature furniture

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Peter is shocked when Duncan calls with news that scandalous photos of his youngest daughter Lily are about to hit the newspapers, while suspicions arise within his family that he has not been entirely faithful. In Washington DC, Charmian Pepper resumes her investigation into Peter's dealings and makes a breakthrough - but with terrible consequences

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium, as the Foxes looked to avenge a defeat at the hands of the Gunners at King Power Stadium in the Carabao Cup. Plus, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of novelist Andrea Levy, charting her journey to become one of the country's best loved contemporary novelists. He looks back at her best-selling novel, Small Island, which captured the imagination of readers around the world, and her most recent novel, The Long Song, has recently been adapted by the BBC, featuring an all-star cast

programme.desc: Ian MacKinnon presents hymns, psalms and personal faith stories. Plus, the Glasgow Islay Trio sing Fulangas Chrìosd, James Graham performs Psalm 100, Gemma Steele explains the role her beliefs play in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Air an Smùid (Steaming)
programme.desc: A team of puffer enthusiasts battles to save the rusty, corroded VIC 27 from the scrap heap. On her rusty bow she bears the name Vital Spark, a throwback to her glory days as star of the BBC's 1994 series The Para Handy Tales. The team members look on nervously as she's carefully winched up the purpose built slipway. This is the first time in almost 30 years she's been out of the water, and at last they can assess the condition of her hull prior to ferrying her by canal to Crinan where her restoration begins

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Fiona Crowden visits a horse in Forfar with ongoing breathing problems, a Chihuahua needs surgery on a sensitive part of his body and it's lambing season for Ella Hamilton

programme.title: Bha mi Latha Samhriadh (When Stornoway Was Dancing)
programme.desc: A warm-hearted look back at aspects of the social life of Stornoway following the Second World War. It was a time of enormous change, with increasing wealth and a new culture imposing itself through music and film, but for a few halcyon years the baby-boomers uniquely enjoyed the best of both worlds

programme.desc: A unique filmic glimpse into the lives of islanders inspired by winter in one of Britain's most Northern communities - the Outer Hebrides

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents a music series for our times, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans

programme.title: Sàr Sgeòil - As the Women Lay Dreaming
programme.desc: Donald S Murray's novel As the Women Lay Dreaming is one of the first works of fiction to explore the 1919 Iolaire tragedy in which over 200 men were lost, the majority being islanders returning home from the Great War. Cathy MacDonald explores the fact behind the fiction

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads prepare dishes using gannet, from dehydrating the meat to use as seasoning in a beef stroganoff to making pasties and pate

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks Weasel has opened a restaurant, and wants to help him improve it. He mistakes the FizzLab for a kitchen!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: When Magoo treats Mr Cat to a parachute jump, the two friends land on the top of the AeroFizz. Mr Magoo mistakes it for a desert island!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Weasel (in an animal costume) for a groundhog being hunted, and confiscates the hunter's gun.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: International Rescue try to save a geological survey team from a huge mining vehicle and its enigmatic driver. With the voices of Jenna Coleman and Thomas Brodie-Sangster

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: CITV's Dare Master at Home sees our Dare Master, Danny Murphy, tackle three challenges per episode that you can try for yourself. As always, the cream pie forfeit is waiting should he fail to complete the challenges! The 10-part entertainment series is a scaled down version of Dare Master produced by ITV SignPost under social distancing filming restrictions due to Covid-19.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Rick and Ginger leave town with instructions for Tom to keep the house in order, but Jerry's got other ideas

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: An author re-visits the hotel, not realising that the kids cleaned up after a recent party and accidentally got rid of the ghost author who really writes his books!

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: Guests dropping into the kitchen include Katie Melua and chefs Dipna Anand and Stephen Terry. James meets up with Grace Dent to go on a culinary tour of Birmingham, gives a masterclass in jam-making and prepares another recipe on the banks of the River Test

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares meals for a lazy Sunday, joined by Gyles Brandreth, Henry Firth, Ian Theasby and Candice Brown. On the menu are sweetcorn fritters served with avocado, tomato and black bean salsa and poached eggs, custard tarts, bloody Mary with polenta mozzarella sticks, and rosemary and anchovy slow roasted lamb with roasted roots, while Gyles contributes his take on the fish finger sandwich

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Italy (Kick-off 3.30pm). Coverage of both teams' fourth match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium, as the competition resumes after a break of more than seven months. Ireland had won both their home fixtures, overcoming Scotland and Wales, but they did lose their last match against England at Twickenham. However, they will be expecting to get back to winning ways today as the Italians have not won a Six Nations contest since 2015. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Rory Best, Brian O'Driscoll and Maggie Alphonsi, commentary by Miles Harrison, Gordon D'Arcy and Danielle Waterman, and reports from Gabriel Clarke

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking Rob Beckett, Ruth Jones and Jordan Banjo to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts a celebrity edition of the game show in which Stacey Dooley and Kevin Clifton try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £1,000,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined in the studio by guests comedian Russell Howard, classical singer Katherine Jenkins and Line of Duty star Daniel Mays. Rapper Dizzee Rascal will be chatting about his career and performing live in the studio

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: World heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua and comedians Kevin Bridges and Jon Richardson join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh for physical games and head-to-head quizzes. Regular panellist Romesh Ranganathan lines up alongside Lampard, with Seann Walsh serving as resident score-keeper

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts a celebrity edition of the game show in which Stacey Dooley and Kevin Clifton try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £1,000,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the sixth episode, the Goodman family from Nottinghamshire and the Ali family from Manchester battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Alan and John answer a distress call from an eerie abandoned space station. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Thomas Brodie-Sangster and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: Dare Master at Home

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp are joined by actors Ben Hardy and Tamzin Outhwaite and comedian Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin takes a canoe journey along the River Wye, meandering along the border of England and Wales where the river offers up a wealth of wildlife including one of Britain's most elusive and mysterious birds - the night jar. Ben Fogle heads to the Exmoor coast and the picturesque village of Porlock, where he samples the locally farmed oysters that are making a comeback. Plus, the programme revisits Britain's biggest farming family, the Owens in the Yorkshire Dales, to see how baby number nine Nancy is settling in

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: The host chats to Shirley Ballas, Jeremy Vine and Shaun Wallace. Farm vet Theeb also introduces Alan to a pair of rams, and a chainsaw-carver gets to work on something special. Plus, with no tennis taking place at Wimbledon this summer, Alan looks back at his own VIP tour of their precious turf

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford explore the biodiverse world of Kew Gardens in London. Plus, a glimpse of the galaxy from Kielder Observatory in Northumberland

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on some of the most memorable coastal gardens the team has created in the show, including a Croatian-inspired coastal escape, a garden with a flavour of the Mediterranean, and a sea adventure-themed family garden with relaxing beachside spaces for the adults

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares meals for a lazy Sunday, joined by Gyles Brandreth, Henry Firth, Ian Theasby and Candice Brown. On the menu are sweetcorn fritters served with avocado, tomato and black bean salsa and poached eggs, custard tarts, bloody Mary with polenta mozzarella sticks, and rosemary and anchovy slow roasted lamb with roasted roots, while Gyles contributes his take on the fish finger sandwich

programme.title: Babe: Pig in the City (1998)
programme.desc: The pig and his farmyard pals take a trip to the big city, where their curiosity and love of adventure lands them in trouble, leaving the courageous Babe to find a way to save his chums from danger. Family comedy sequel, starring Magda Szubanski, Mickey Rooney and James Cromwell, and featuring the voice of Elizabeth Daily as Babe

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection filmed by viewers, including a toddler terrified by a clam, a cyclist falling into a filthy canal and a teenager accidentally hitting his dad with a sledgehammer

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Paddy, Vicky, Mat and Steph pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Jeff Stelling, Angela Rippon and Matt Edmondson to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the game show in which the Wilson family from Worcester take on the Popat family from Harrow. The family who score the most points go through to Big Money, which sees two of them answering just five questions for the £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Having just turned 80, Cliff Richard joins Piers Morgan to look back on his life and six-decade career. He recalls his journey from a poverty-stricken childhood, how he became Britain's first rock-and-roll star, and at one stage the biggest selling singles artist in the world

programme.title: The Olivier Awards
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts from The London Palladium, celebrating the best of British theatre and featuring performances from nominated musicals & Juliet, and Dear Evan Hansen. Other nominees include Hayley Atwell, Sharon D Clarke, Juliet Stevenson and Phoebe Waller-Bridge, who are all up for Best Actress, and Toby Jones, James McAvoy, Wendell Pierce and Andrew Scott vying for Best Actor. Lyricist Don Black will be one of two award winners also being honoured

programme.title: Goodwood Speedweek Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the motoring event from West Sussex, which brought together elements of the Festival of Speed and the Revival following the cancellation of those events. Plus, a selection of races only usually seen at the Members' Meetings

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents from Oulton Park, featuring round four of the Ginetta GT5 Challenge and round six of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents highlights of the sixth and final round of this year's Bennetts' British Superbikes Championship. Three races were scheduled to take place at the world-famous circuit, where one rider would be crowned champion for 2020

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five people who are family or friends answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Steve, Alex, Jane and Terry pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dan unwittingly puts Amelia in danger, Meena receives a surprise gift, and Nate is hopeful

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A masked Scott bursts into the bistro and threatens everyone with a gun, while Steve breaks the news to Leanne that he's tried 10 more specialists but no one is willing to fight their corner. Elsewhere, Imran suggests to Alya they might have to sell Yasmeen's house to pay her legal fees

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: A Jack Russell is taken by ambulance to the Hospital in London Victoria as its elderly owner is worried her pet is unable to control its bladder, while a Chihuahua is in theatre with Maria for a delicate knee operation. In Grimsby, a blind rescue husky is checked in for the day to have its diabetes monitored, and a stray cat is the 200th to be brought to the hospital in a year, but is in need of urgent treatment before the charity can find it a new home

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Scott orders Ray and his mates to hand over their cash, and when Alya runs a credit check on Yasmeen, she is shocked to see a series of unpaid loans against her name. Over dinner, Todd tries to belittle George, but he gives Grimshaw short shrift

programme.title: The Sister
programme.desc: Nathan Redman has spent years trying to atone for a terrible event from his past and is now a decade into his marriage, but one rainy evening he is surprised by an unwelcome visit by a figure from his old life. Murder mystery, starring Russell Tovey, Bertie Carvel and Amanda Root

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: First Among Equals: The Laurie Cunningham Story
programme.desc: The remarkable career of the footballer who became the first Englishman to transfer to Real Madrid and the first black player to represent England in a competitive match. Cunningham inspired a generation of black youngsters before his untimely death at the age of 33 in 1989. Featuring contributions by Ron Atkinson, John Barnes, John Fashanu, Paul Ince and Ian Wright among others

programme.title: The Sister
programme.desc: Nathan Redman has spent years trying to atone for a terrible event from his past and is now a decade into his marriage, but one rainy evening he is surprised by an unwelcome visit by a figure from his old life. Murder mystery, starring Russell Tovey, Bertie Carvel and Amanda Root

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: The arrival of spring sees the crowds return to Snowdon, with railwayman Steff re-opening the summit cafe and singer Shirley Bassey unveiling a train carriage named in her honour. The return of paying customers is good news for Alwena and her cafe 1,800ft up the mountain, but the busy paths to the summit spell trouble for Rob and his mountain rescue team

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks Weasel has opened a restaurant, and wants to help him improve it. He mistakes the FizzLab for a kitchen!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: When Magoo treats Mr Cat to a parachute jump, the two friends land on the top of the AeroFizz. Mr Magoo mistakes it for a desert island!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Weasel (in an animal costume) for a groundhog being hunted, and confiscates the hunter's gun.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: International Rescue try to save a geological survey team from a huge mining vehicle and its enigmatic driver. With the voices of Jenna Coleman and Thomas Brodie-Sangster

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: CITV's Dare Master at Home sees our Dare Master, Danny Murphy, tackle three challenges per episode that you can try for yourself. As always, the cream pie forfeit is waiting should he fail to complete the challenges! The 10-part entertainment series is a scaled down version of Dare Master produced by ITV SignPost under social distancing filming restrictions due to Covid-19.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Rick and Ginger leave town with instructions for Tom to keep the house in order, but Jerry's got other ideas

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: An author re-visits the hotel, not realising that the kids cleaned up after a recent party and accidentally got rid of the ghost author who really writes his books!

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: Guests dropping into the kitchen include Katie Melua and chefs Dipna Anand and Stephen Terry. James meets up with Grace Dent to go on a culinary tour of Birmingham, gives a masterclass in jam-making and prepares another recipe on the banks of the River Test

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares meals for a lazy Sunday, joined by Gyles Brandreth, Henry Firth, Ian Theasby and Candice Brown. On the menu are sweetcorn fritters served with avocado, tomato and black bean salsa and poached eggs, custard tarts, bloody Mary with polenta mozzarella sticks, and rosemary and anchovy slow roasted lamb with roasted roots, while Gyles contributes his take on the fish finger sandwich

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Italy (Kick-off 3.30pm). Coverage of both teams' fourth match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium, as the competition resumes after a break of more than seven months. Ireland had won both their home fixtures, overcoming Scotland and Wales, but they did lose their last match against England at Twickenham. However, they will be expecting to get back to winning ways today as the Italians have not won a Six Nations contest since 2015. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Rory Best, Brian O'Driscoll and Maggie Alphonsi, commentary by Miles Harrison, Gordon D'Arcy and Danielle Waterman, and reports from Gabriel Clarke

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking Rob Beckett, Ruth Jones and Jordan Banjo to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts a celebrity edition of the game show in which Stacey Dooley and Kevin Clifton try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £1,000,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined in the studio by guests comedian Russell Howard, classical singer Katherine Jenkins and Line of Duty star Daniel Mays. Rapper Dizzee Rascal will be chatting about his career and performing live in the studio

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: World heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua and comedians Kevin Bridges and Jon Richardson join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh for physical games and head-to-head quizzes. Regular panellist Romesh Ranganathan lines up alongside Lampard, with Seann Walsh serving as resident score-keeper

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the sixth episode, the Goodman family from Nottinghamshire and the Ali family from Manchester battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Drama about the secrets and dishonour among the upper echelons of 19th-century London society, adapted by Julian Fellowes from his novel of the same name. The year is 1836 and the Trenchard family have recently ascended to the aristocratic society of London's Belgravia - only for a 26-year-old love affair to come back to haunt them and jeopardise the happiness of many. Starring Tamsin Greig, Philip Glenister and Harriet Walter

programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mary investigates the noises in Eileen's loft, David dreads taking sole responsibility for Shona, and Gary convinces Ray that he could be an asset to him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Drama about the secrets and dishonour among the upper echelons of 19th-century London society, adapted by Julian Fellowes from his novel of the same name. The year is 1836 and the Trenchard family have recently ascended to the aristocratic society of London's Belgravia - only for a 26-year-old love affair to come back to haunt them and jeopardise the happiness of many. Starring Tamsin Greig, Philip Glenister and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne decides that honesty is required, and hopes that seeking confession with Caroline will appease her guilt and lay to rest the ghosts from her past. However, she has sorely underestimated Lady Brockenhurst, who sets about unearthing the secrets that bind the families together. Hosting a prestigious dinner party, she invites a special guest whose presence reveals a deceit James Trenchard would wish to keep private

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into the strange murder of eminent art historian Professor Van Haren, who was really a former high-ranking Nazi hired by MI5 due to his expert knowledge of Soviet intelligence. Meanwhile, as Sam's husband Adam embarks on a busy career as an MP, she worries whether she will be able to share some big news with him. Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Former EastEnder and The Bill bad cop Todd Carty guest stars in this festive special of the rural drama. He plays an old army pal of Lord Ashfordly who moves into the hall while the peer is sunning himself in Bermuda, claiming he's there to keep an eye on the place after rumours of a planned break-in. However, he arouses the police's suspicions when he becomes involved with a teenage tearaway who seems to think Christmas isn't so much about giving and receiving as taking without the owner's permission. Elsewhere in the village, Blaketon and Peggy go into competition with rival Santa's grottos, and Ventress has his nose put out of joint when his friends draw straws to see who'll invite him to Christmas dinner while his wife's in hospital

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the match at Stade de France, as the sides prepare for their matches in the final round of Six Nations fixtures at home to Ireland and Scotland respectively. France claimed a 27-23 away win when the sides met in the championship in February, avenging a 20-19 defeat in the quarter-finals of last year's World Cup. With analysis from Gareth Thomas and James Hook, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Shane Williams

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage five, featuring a 185.5km route from Huesca to Sabinanigo. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: The World's End (2013)
programme.desc: A 40-year-old man who refuses to grow up drags four reluctant childhood friends back to their home town to recreate a pub crawl they attempted in their teens. As they work their way round the 12 local bars, they stumble on a threat to the entire human race. Sci-fi comedy, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost and Martin Freeman

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents from Oulton Park, featuring round four of the Ginetta GT5 Challenge and round six of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the sixth weekend of the season as teams headed to Sardinia. Rugged and sun-baked gravel tracks in the north of the picture postcard Mediterranean island ensure one of the season's sternest challenges. Fast and narrow stages leave no margin for error, while intense heat combines with abrasive roads to test tyres to the limit

programme.title: DTM Highlights 2020
programme.desc: The final third of the 2020 DTM season takes the series back to Belgium, this time to Circuit Zolder in Round 7. The venue, built in 1963, actually hosted the very first race in DTM history, and was brought back into the calendar in 2019. Described as an old-school drivers' circuit, the layout of Zolder guarantees exciting races with little margin for error. Eventual champion Rene Rast and Philipp Eng were winners here last year

programme.title: Live British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Steve Rider and Louis Goodman present coverage of the 22nd, 23rd and 24th races of the season, which come from Snetterton. This is the penultimate meeting of the campaign and Ashley Sutton currently leads the standings, but with the likes of Dan Cammish and Colin Turkington breathing down his neck. With commentary by Tim Harvey, Richard John Neil and Paul O'Neill

programme.title: Goodwood Speedweek Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the motoring event from West Sussex, which brought together elements of the Festival of Speed and the Revival following the cancellation of those events. Plus, a selection of races only usually seen at the Members' Meetings

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Liverpool
programme.desc: A look at Liverpool's four European Cup triumphs between 1977 and 1984, charting how manager Bob Paisley helped them to become a European superpower after his predecessor Bill Shankly had laid the foundations. With highlights of the finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage six, featuring a 136.6km route from Biesca to Col du Tourmalet. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Coventry City v Tottenham Hotspur. Ian St John and Jimmy Greaves hosted coverage of the 1987 FA Cup final at the old Wembley Stadium, which proved to be one of the most memorable showpiece matches in the competition's history. With an introduction by Gabriel Clarke and commentary from Brian Moore

programme.title: GoldenEye (1995)
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the match at Stade de France, as the sides prepare for their matches in the final round of Six Nations fixtures at home to Ireland and Scotland respectively. France claimed a 27-23 away win when the sides met in the championship in February, avenging a 20-19 defeat in the quarter-finals of last year's World Cup. With analysis from Gareth Thomas and James Hook, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Shane Williams

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage five, featuring a 185.5km route from Huesca to Sabinanigo. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: The World's End (2013)
programme.desc: A 40-year-old man who refuses to grow up drags four reluctant childhood friends back to their home town to recreate a pub crawl they attempted in their teens. programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the sixth weekend of the season as teams headed to Sardinia. Rugged and sun-baked gravel tracks in the north of the picture postcard Mediterranean island ensure one of the season's sternest challenges. Fast and narrow stages leave no margin for error, while intense heat combines with abrasive roads to test tyres to the limit

programme.title: DTM Highlights 2020
programme.desc: The final third of the 2020 DTM season takes the series back to Belgium, this time to Circuit Zolder in Round 7. The venue, built in 1963, actually hosted the very first race in DTM history, and was brought back into the calendar in 2019. Described as an old-school drivers' circuit, the layout of Zolder guarantees exciting races with little margin for error. Eventual champion Rene Rast and Philipp Eng were winners here last year

programme.title: Live British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Steve Rider and Louis Goodman present coverage of the 22nd, 23rd and 24th races of the season, which come from Snetterton. This is the penultimate meeting of the campaign and Ashley Sutton currently leads the standings, but with the likes of Dan Cammish and Colin Turkington breathing down his neck. With commentary by Tim Harvey, Richard John Neil and Paul O'Neill

programme.title: Goodwood Speedweek Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the motoring event from West Sussex, which brought together elements of the Festival of Speed and the Revival following the cancellation of those events. Plus, a selection of races only usually seen at the Members' Meetings

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Liverpool
programme.desc: A look at Liverpool's four European Cup triumphs between 1977 and 1984, charting how manager Bob Paisley helped them to become a European superpower after his predecessor Bill Shankly had laid the foundations. With highlights of the finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage six, featuring a 136.6km route from Biesca to Col du Tourmalet. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Coventry City v Tottenham Hotspur. Ian St John and Jimmy Greaves hosted coverage of the 1987 FA Cup final at the old Wembley Stadium, which proved to be one of the most memorable showpiece matches in the competition's history. With an introduction by Gabriel Clarke and commentary from Brian Moore

programme.title: GoldenEye (1995)
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale

Shows they are appraising include Simply Nigella, Five Guys a Week, The Andrew Marr Show interview with Michael Gove, Family Fortunes, Scream, The Million Pound Cube, Emily's Story: Stand Up to Cancer, and news coverage of Manchaster mayor Andy Burnham's skirmish with the Government

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm refuses to tarnish the reputation of a promotion rival at work, while Frasier tries to awaken Diane's romantic feelings with a meticulously planned slide show. Bar-room comedy, starring George Wendt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert bumps into his ex-wife, and considers rekindling the relationship. Meanwhile, Frank tells Amy the story of how the pair split up in the first place

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Marie surprises everyone by arranging a trip to Rome for the whole family, but all Ray can do is complain. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of two. As the family's Roman holiday continues, Robert falls in love with sultry local beauty Stefania (Alex Meneses), who works in an ice-cream shop

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin suffers a mild heart attack, but tries to keep it from Ronee - to the dismay of Frasier and Niles, who feel it would be unfair of him to hide such serious news. Eventually worn down by their persistence, he decides to confide in her - leading to a conversation that ends in an unexpected way

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Infuriated at the dullness of his life, Frasier steals a small box from a neighbour for the thrill, but guilt prompts him to look for a way to return it without being caught - a task that proves easier said than done. With the guest voice of Helen Mirren

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Todd Pedersen, CEO and founder of Vivint, a leading solar and home security business, based in Utah, goes undercover among his own workforce posing as a contestant on a reality show. He's proud to head an organisation that provides a fun and relaxed work environment, but now that the company is offering smart home technology, it's in competition with much larger organisations, and Todd wants to make sure it's fully prepared to take them on

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part edition, Gordon Ramsay heads to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, an Italian restaurant run by sisters Rita and Lisa. The pair grew up working at their father's eatery nearby, but when they opened their own business, they couldn't replicate his success, and they now blame their failure on everything from the weather to the tablecloths

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with food critic Jay Rayner alongside Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: The Great House Giveaway

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
